mnemonics: SPACE Helps Recall Vector Rules: Span, Properties, Axioms, Closure, Examples
keyPoints: A vector space is a collection of vectors closed under vector addition and scalar multiplication.
It must satisfy ten specific axioms, including associativity, commutativity, and distributivity.
Familiar examples include R^n, matrices of a given size, and sets of polynomials.
commonMistakes: Assuming any collection of vectors is a vector space without checking all ten axioms.
Confusing the requirements for closure under addition and scalar multiplication.
